steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: ‘Indonesia is required _____ other Interpol countries with access to its law enforcement databases.’ The passive construction ‘is required’ governs the blank. We need the verb form that correctly follows ‘required.’

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘provided’: past participle, not an infinitive. ‘Is required provided’ is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘to provide’: infinitive. ‘Required to provide’ is the standard construction — ‘required’ always takes an infinitive complement. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘providing’: gerund/present participle. ‘Is required providing’ is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘provides’: finite verb, creating a second main clause without a conjunction. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
